title: 第 59 節
en: 2. The Teacher replies : ' It is the ear of the ear, the mind of the mind, the speech of speech, the breath of breath, and the eye of the eye. When freed (from the senses) the wise, on departing from this world, become immortal \ 3. ' The eye does not go thither, nor speech, nor mind. We do not know, we do not understand, how any one can teach it.
4. 'It is different from the known, it is also above the unknown, thus we have heard from those of old, who taught us this 2.

5. ' That which is not expressed by speech and 1 This verse admits of various translations, and still more various explanations. Instead of taking va<£am, like all the other words, as a nominative, we might take them all as accusatives, governed by atimuXya, and sa u pra;zasya pvanak as a parenthetical sentence. What is meant by the ear of the ear is very fully explained by the commentator, but the simplest acceptation would seem to take it as an answer to the preceding questions, so that the ear of the ear should be taken for him who directs the ear, i. e. the Self, or Brahman. This will become clearer as we proceed.
2 Cf. Ira Up. 11 ; 13.
by which speech is expressed, that alone know as Brahman, not that which people here adore.
6. ' That which does not think by mind, and by which, they say, mind is thought1, that alone know as Brahman, not that which people here adore.
7. ' That which does not see by the eye, and by which one sees (the work of) the eyes, that alone know as Brahman, not that which people here adore.
8. ' That which does not hear by the ear, and by which the ear is heard, that alone know as Brahman, not that which people here adore.
9. ' That which does not breathe by breath, and by which breath is drawn, that alone know as Brahman, not that which people here adore.'
Second Khanda.
1. The Teacher says: 'If thou thinkest I know it well, then thou knowest surely but little, what is that form of Brahman known, it may be, to thee 2 ? '

2. The Pupil says : ' I do not think I know it well, nor do I know that I do not know it. He 1 The varia lectio manaso matam (supported also by the commentary) is metrically and grammatically easier, but it may be, for that very reason, an emendation.

2 In order to obtain a verse, we must leave out the words tvam yad asya deveshv atha nu mimawsyam eva. They were probably inserted, as an excuse for the third kha«ifa treating of the relation of Brahman to the Devas. There is considerable variety in the text, as handed down in the Sama-veda and in the Atharva-veda, which shows that it has been tampered with. Daharam for dabhram may be the older reading, as synezesis occurs again and again in the Upanishads.

Ill KHANDA, I. 149 among us who knows this, he knows it, nor does he know that he does not know it K 3. ' He by whom it (Brahman) is not thought, by him it is thought ; he by whom it is thought, knows it not. It is not understood by those who understand it, it is understood by those who do not understand it.
4. ' It is thought to be known (as if) by awakening, and (then) we obtain immortality indeed. By the Self we obtain strength, by knowledge we obtain immortality.

5. ' If a man know this here, that is the true (end of life) ; if he does not know this here, then there is great destruction (new births). The wise who have thought on all things (and recognised the Self in them) become immortal, when they have departed from this world.'
Third Khanda2.

1. Brahman obtained the victory for the Devas. The Devas became elated by the victory of Brah- 1 This verse has again been variously explained. I think the train of thought is this : We cannot know Brahman, as we know other objects, by referring them to a class and pointing out their differences. But, on the other hand, we do not know that we know him not, i. e. no one can assert that we know him not, for we want Brahman in order to know anything. He, therefore, who knows this double peculiarity of the knowledge of Brahman, he knows Brahman, as much as it can be known; and he does not know, nor can anybody prove it to him, that he does not know Brahman.

This khanda is generally represented as a later addition, but its prose style has more of a Brahmawa character than the verses in the preceding kkandas, although their metrical structure is irregular, and may be taken as a sign of antiquity.
man, and they thought, this victory is ours only, this greatness is ours only.
2. Brahman perceived this and appeared to them. But they did not know it, and said : ' What sprite (yaksha or yakshya) is this ? '
3. They said to Agni (fire) : 'O Catavedas, find out what sprite this is.' ' Yes,' he said.
4. He ran toward it, and Brahman said to him : ' Who are you ? ' He replied : ' I am Agni, I am ^atavedas.'
5. Brahman said : ' What power is in you ? ' Agni replied : ' I could burn all whatever there is on earth.'

6. Brahman put a straw before him, saying: ' Burn this.' He went towards it with all his might, but he could not burn it. Then he returned thence and said : ' I could not find out what sprite this is.'
7. Then they said to Vayu (air) : 'O Vayu, find out what sprite this is.' ' Yes,' he said.
8. He ran toward it, and Brahman said to him : 'Who are you?' He replied: 'I am Vayu, I am Matariivan.'
9. Brahman said : 'What power is in you ? ' Vayu replied : ' I could take up all whatever there is on earth.'

10. Brahman put a straw before him, saying : ' Take it up.' He went towards it with all his might, but he could not take it up. Then he returned thence and said : ' I could not find out what sprite this is.'
11. Then they said to Indra : 'O Maghavan, find out what sprite this is.' He went towards it, but it disappeared from before him.
zh: 2. 教師回答：「它是耳之耳、心之心、語之語、息之息、眼之眼。當智者解脫（於感官）而離開此世時，便成為不朽者。」3. 「眼不往彼處，語亦不往，心亦不往。我們不知道，我們不理解，任何人如何能教導它。
4. 「它異於已知之物，亦超越未知之物；如此我們才得以聞知，乃從昔日的先賢那裡，彼等曾教導我們此理。」²

5. '那不能被言語表達的，以及¹ ¹ 這一詩句可以有多種譯法，解釋更是繁多。與其像所有其他詞語一樣把 vāçam 作為主格，我們可以把它們全部作為受格，受 atimukya 支配，而 sa u prajñasya pranaḥ 作為插入句。「耳之耳」（the ear of the ear）的含義在注疏家的詳盡說明中得到充分解釋，但最簡單的用法似乎應當把它視為對前述問題的回答，使得「耳之耳」應理解為指引耳朵者，即自我（Self）或梵（Brahman）。隨著我們的進展，這將變得更加清晰。
2 參見《伊剌奧義書》11、13。
人們必須認識梵天（Brahman），乃在於表達言語的那個原則，而非世人在此地所膜拜的那個。
那不是用心思而思的東西，卻是那使心思被思考的東西，正如他們所說的，那唯一的就是梵（Brahman），而非人們在此世所崇拜的東西。 --- 1 原註：此句出自《肯那奧義書》（Kena Upanishad）。
那不以眼睛看，卻使人能看（眼睛的作用），唯獨那個才是梵天（Brahman），而非此間人們所崇拜的東西。
8. 「那不為耳所聞，而耳由其而聞者，唯此應知為梵（Brahman），非世人此處所崇拜者。」
9. 「那不以呼吸而呼吸，以及藉由它而呼吸被吸取者，唯獨認識那為梵天，非此處人們所崇拜者。」
第二章
師父說：「若汝以為我深知此事，則汝確實所知甚少。那婆羅門（Brahman）的形相究竟為何，汝或許自知其詳？」
2. 弟子說：「我不認為我知道它，我也不認為我不知道它。 ¹ *manaso matam* 的異文讀法（也得到注疏的支持）在韻律和文法上都較為容易，但正因為如此，它可能是一個後人的修訂。

為了獲得詩句，我們必須刪除「tvam yad asya deveshv atha nu mimawsyam eva」這些詞語。它們可能是被插入的，作為第三章（khanda）討論梵天（Brahman）與提婆（Devas）關係的藉口。在《娑摩吠陀》（Sama-veda）和《阿闥婆吠陀》（Atharva-veda）中傳承的文本呈現相當的差異，這表明它曾遭到篡改。「daharam」可能是比「dabhram」更古老的讀法，因為綴合變化（synezesis）在《奧義書》中一再出現。
第三卷，第一經，149 條　我們中間誰知道這個，他就知道它，也不知道他不知道它 K 3。「那些不思念它（梵）的人反而思念了它；那些思念它的人反而不知道它。它不被那些理解它的人所理解，卻被那些不理解它的人所理解。
4. 『據認為它（仿佛）通過覺醒而被認知，然後我們確實獲得了不朽。藉由自我（Self）我們獲得力量，藉由知識我們獲得不朽。
5. 「若人於此處知曉此事，乃是真正（生命的目的）；若人於此處不知曉此事，則有大毀滅（新的輪迴）。智者們思慮萬物（並在其中認知自我）者，當彼等離此世界時，成為不朽。」
第三分章²

梵天為提婆們獲得了勝利。提婆們因梵天的勝利而欣喜若狂。 --- **註腳譯文：** 1 這首詩句又被作過各種各樣的解釋。我認為思想的脈絡是這樣的：我們不能像認識其他對象那樣認識梵天（Brahman），即通過將其歸入某一類別並指出其區別特徵而認識它。但另一方面，我們並不知道我們不認識它，也就是說，沒有人能斷言我們不認識它，因為我們需要梵天來認識任何事物。因此，凡是知悉梵天的知識之這種雙重特殊性的人，他就認識了梵天，就其能被認識的程度而言；而且他不知道，任何人也不能向他證明，他不認識梵天。
這個凱達（khanda）通常被認為是後來的增補，但其散文風格比前述凱達的詩句更具有婆羅門書（Brahmawa）的特徵，儘管這些詩句的韻律結構並不規則，而這可被視為古老性的一個標誌。
人類，他們認為，這勝利只屬於我們，這偉大只屬於我們。
2. 梵天（Brahman）察覺到這一點，於是向他們顯現。但他們不認識，說道：「這是什麼精靈（yaksha 或 yakshya）呢？」
3. 他們對阿格尼（火）說：『哦，察塔吠陀，找出這是什麼精靈。』『好的，』他說。
4. 他向它跑去，梵天對他說：「你是誰？」他回答：「我是阿格尼，我是^atavedas。」
5. 梵天說：「你有什麼力量？」阿格尼回答：「我能燒盡地上的一切。」
梵天在他面前放了一根稻草，說：「燒掉這個。」他使盡全力走向它，但他無法燒掉它。然後他從那裡回來，說：「我無法查明這是什麼精靈。」
他們隨後對瓦尤（氣）說：「瓦尤啊，去查明這是什麼精靈。」「好的，」他說。
他跑向它，梵天對他說：「你是誰？」他回答說：「我是伐由，我是摩多梨濕縛（Matariivan）。」
梵天說：「你有什麼力量？」風神回答：「我能夠拿起地上所有一切的東西。」
10. 梵天在他面前放了一根稻草，說：「把它拿起來。」他竭盡全力走向它，但卻無法將其拿起。然後他返回，說：「我找不出這是什麼精靈。」
11. 於是他們對因陀羅說：「哦，摩罕婆，查明這個精靈是什麼。」他向它走去，但它在他面前消失了。
